title: rosa canina subvar. spuria
intro: Rosa canina subvar. spuria, commonly known as the wild rose, is a shrub-like plant belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly due to its high content of vitamin C, flavonoids, and anthocyanins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rosa canina subvar. spuria include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its ability to support immune function and act as an antimicrobial agent. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections.
